confidence_rationale: Clinical-pathological corroboration of the precision-weighting framework: hallucinations, delusions, sensory-attenuation deficits, catatonia, and abnormal eye movements are all unified as expressions of aberrant precision. The paper supplies an indirect failure-mode prediction that is highly relevant to MECH-269b's wired-but-inert hypothesis: when precision on prediction-error channels is mis-set, downstream consumers receive technically-correct but contextually-wrong PE signals, and behavioural revision is impaired in characteristic ways. Tag (c) inferential support plus tag (d) failure-mode evidence. Confidence 0.66 reflects clinical-mapping caveats (the syndromes are heterogeneous and the precision model is one of several competing accounts) and the fact that the paper does not directly measure per-stream precision in psychosis or the hippocampal-cortical symmetry.
failure_signatures: Adams et al. argue that aberrant precision on sensory or prior beliefs produces specific psychopathological syndromes (hallucinations from over-precise priors, delusions from systematic precision errors, catatonia from collapsed precision on motor priors). This is a failure-mode prediction that supports the MECH-269b architectural claim by showing what happens when precision is mis-set; but it is indirect support, not direct measurement.

The paper is largely silent on whether the same precision parameter that mis-fires in psychosis is used by the hippocampal proposer. Hippocampus is mentioned briefly (in the context of memory and pattern completion) but the symmetric-application question is not engaged.

Clinical mapping is heterogeneous: not all psychotic patients have the same precision profile, and the precision-weighting account competes with dopamine-signal-to-noise and aberrant-salience accounts. The supportive force is therefore at the framework level, not at the level of any single empirical measurement.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A range of psychotic symptoms (hallucinosis, abnormal smooth-pursuit eye movements, sensory-attenuation deficits, catatonia, delusions) can be unified as expressions of aberrant precision on prediction errors and prior beliefs in hierarchical predictive coding. Precision is biologically encoded by post-synaptic gain (NMDA-receptor function, dopaminergic neuromodulation), and aberrant precision produces behaviours that fail to revise in light of accumulating counter-evidence -- because the prediction errors that should drive revision are themselves down-weighted.
mapping.ree_translation: Adams et al. supply the failure-mode prediction that grounds MECH-269b's architectural-prediction half: when V_s is mis-set across cortical streams (the pathology of psychosis), downstream consumers receive technically-correct prediction-error inputs but those inputs do not weight the contribution of unreliable streams correctly, and behavioural revision is suppressed. The wired-but-inert failure mode that MECH-269b's parent claim warns about ("stale-but-confident-looking prediction errors that suppress revision") is exactly the clinical phenotype Adams et al. describe under aberrant precision.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Clinical heterogeneity. Not all psychotic patients fit the precision-weighting model, and the model competes with dopamine-signal-to-noise and aberrant-salience accounts. Also, the paper does not directly measure per-stream precision -- the support is framework-level, predicting that mis-set precision produces specific failure modes, which inferentially backs the architectural claim that precision is the relevant gating signal in the first place.
mapping.source_context: Frontiers in Psychiatry review by Friston, Frith, and clinical-computational collaborators. Bridges the theoretical predictive-coding framework with clinical psychopathology.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality good (peer-reviewed, senior authorship, broad clinical synthesis). Mapping fidelity moderate -- the failure-mode prediction is indirect support for the architectural claim. Transfer risk noticeable because clinical psychosis is heterogeneous and the precision account is one of several competing frameworks.
